args.existingTab: true or explicit policy to claim a user-open Chrome tab instead of opening a replacement
args.prompt: message to send or workflow-specific input

examples: const agent = chatgpt.agent({ name: "reviewer", instructions: "Review deeply." }); await chatgpt.runner.run(agent, { input: "Review this.", thread: { type: "new" }, experience: "chat" });
await chatgpt.runner.run(agent, { input: "Continue.", thread: { type: "url", url: "https://chatgpt.com/c/<conversation-id>" }, existingTab: true });
name: runner.run
retryPolicy: Return structured CommandResult failures; do not resubmit prompts unless the sequence policy permits unmatched-turn recovery.
summary: Agents-style facade: run a visible ChatGPT agent against input, files, thread, existing-tab, experience, configuration, legacy mode, and response options.
